clean_body: Our educational workshops feature acclaimed college professors and successful entrepreneurs who bring the concepts of individual liberty, free enterprise, limited government, personal responsibility, and entrepreneurship to life. Friend, Capitalism is evil, socialism is moral, and you should rely on the government for all your needs: this is what schools are teaching students. Socialists are trying to reinvigorate the disastrous and evil ideas of socialism and indoctrinate the next generation. But we can counter these ideas by introducing students to the ideas of liberty and free markets. The Foundation for Economic Education is taking action with FEE in the Classroom. Let me introduce myself. My name is Zilvinas Silenas, and I am President of the Foundation for Economic Education (FEE). Our mission is to inspire, educate, and connect future leaders with the economic, ethical, and legal principles of a free society. Last year we trained over 55,000 students and teachers in liberty and free market ideas. And with your help, FEE is on track to reach over 65,000 students and teachers this year. To help achieve that goal, we're raising $50,000 by the end of the month. FEE in the Classroom is a unique program held in brick-and-mortar and virtual classrooms across the country that teaches young Americans to think outside the box and challenge the mainstream narrative. Our educational workshops feature acclaimed college professors and successful entrepreneurs who bring the concepts of individual liberty, free enterprise, limited government, personal responsibility, and entrepreneurship to life. In a single day, 75-300 students engage with FEE's faculty of acclaimed instructors through compelling lectures, exciting economic experiments, engaging group discussions, and Q&As. Students learn how the core principles of individual liberty and free-market economics apply to their daily lives, communities, careers, and the state of the nation. These subjects are extremely important in our everyday lives, but the Radical Left doesn't think students are old enough to learn them. A basic understanding of economics is key. The younger generation needs to know how to handle money, understand inflation, and more before they step out on their own. And FEE in the Classroom is a chance for these students to learn those topics. FEE in the Classroom is your opportunity to pass the torch of liberty to thousands of students, Friend. Can I count on you to support FEE in the Classroom and help reach 65,000 students this year? Click below to donate and help reach our goal of raising $50,000 this month. Thank you, Zilvinas Silenas President Foundation for Economic Education FEE is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit educational organization, so donations made to FEE are tax-deductible to the full extent allowable by law. Sent to Conservatives Give subscribers. 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59465321/394329981/227139380?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGY5ODU0YS03NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7YXPIE08cpV13AZXgE7RpYUXpHBRNIJmJ5OtNwn8UU=&emci=b7dcedb3-79b7-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=70f9854a-75b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=43566853)

| | **Peter: I’m officially running for re-election to the U.S. Senate to keep delivering results for Virginia!**

Throughout my life I’ve been honored to serve people as a missionary, civil rights lawyer, and elected official at the local, state and federal levels. I love the Commonwealth of Virginia and its citizens, and I want to keep serving as a Senator for everyone.

[**_There’s a lot of work to do over the next two years to keep our Commonwealth of Virginia blue up and down the ballot, so right now, I’d like to ask you to become a Founding Donor to my campaign and split a donation between my campaign and the Democratic Party of Georgia. I’d be very grateful for whatever support you can give.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59465322/394329982/417786487?refcode=20230301.2_TK_3B&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGY5ODU0YS03NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7YXPIE08cpV13AZXgE7RpYUXpHBRNIJmJ5OtNwn8UU=)**_

**Peter, we’ve accomplished a lot together these last few years:**

We’ve lowered the costs of both health care and energy with the Inflation Reduction Act.

We made the biggest commitment to infrastructure since the Interstate Highway system initiative in the Eisenhower Administration with the Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill.

We brought back American manufacturing jobs with the CHIPS Act.

We protected marriage equality with the Respect for Marriage Act.

And we passed the first major federal legislation in decades that will tackle the scourge of gun violence.

**But there is so much still to be done.**

Because we need more skilled workers, we should invest in job training, provide more affordable high-quality child care, and find a path to a work-based immigration reform bill.

We need to expand access to mental health services, battle addiction, and bring down health care costs for all.

We need to fight for fundamental liberties, including the freedom to make your own reproductive decisions and the right to vote.

Our natural bounty — air, water, open space — needs protection from threats including climate change.

And in a world with threats to democracies at home and abroad, we need a national security strategy that keeps us safe while building up the capacity of the U.S. and our allies to link arms against global threats of all kinds.

**But here’s the thing — all those challenges don’t discourage me from this work._In fact, they are exactly why I’m running to keep representing Virginia in the U.S. Senate._** I will continue to deliver for EVERYONE, listen to your thoughts and concerns, and work across the aisle to solve the problems that keep us up at night. Because in a divided government, we need to work with Democrats _and_ Republicans to get things done.

This isn’t going to be easy. Virginia is a battleground state with a Republican governor, and special interests are going to spend massively to try and block progress in our Commonwealth.

[**_I have to ask: Will you split a donation today between my campaign and another battleground state, the Democratic Party of Georgia, to become a Founding Donor of my campaign?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59465323/394329983/417786487?refcode=20230301.2_TK_3B&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGY5ODU0YS03NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7YXPIE08cpV13AZXgE7RpYUXpHBRNIJmJ5OtNwn8UU=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

[Donate $250 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59465328/394329988/417785495?express_lane=true&refcode=20230301.2_TK_3B&amount=250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGY5ODU0YS03NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7YXPIE08cpV13AZXgE7RpYUXpHBRNIJmJ5OtNwn8UU=)
[Donate another amount >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59465329/394329989/417786487?refcode=20230301.2_TK_3B&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGY5ODU0YS03NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7YXPIE08cpV13AZXgE7RpYUXpHBRNIJmJ5OtNwn8UU=)
**_We’ve got a big fight ahead of us, and we can’t take a single thing for granted._**

I know from experience that this grassroots team can make an incredible difference when we stand up and work together. We can protect our Democratic majority in the Senate, we can help elect more Democrats all the way down the ballot, and we can secure victories for Virginians and all Americans.

[**_Please split a donation today to help kick off my campaign for re-election so we can continue to lead with solutions, not stunts.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59465330/394329990/417786487?refcode=20230301.2_TK_3B&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGY5ODU0YS03NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7YXPIE08cpV13AZXgE7RpYUXpHBRNIJmJ5OtNwn8UU=)**_

Thanks for all your support and guidance over the years.

I hope to earn your support once again to battle for Virginia priorities, champion American innovation, and defend democracy and human rights across the globe.

Thanks so much — let’s get to work!

Tim Kaine

I hope to earn your support once again to battle for Virginia priorities, champion American innovation, and defend democracy and human rights across the globe. Thanks so much — let’s get to work! Tim Kaine | | Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.georgiademocrat.org | Democratic Party of Georgia P.O. BOX 89202 Atlanta, GA 30312 United States

**Kevin was sentenced to three years in prison because he carried a Confederate flag through the U.S. Capitol building on Jan. 6, 2021.**

You'll notice there isn't one word there about the crimes he allegedly committed ... just the flag.

But that didn't save him ... neither did his forced public apology.

What's worse, the federal prosecutor wanted Kevin to serve 70 months - 5 years and 10 months - in prison for his "crimes!"

I worked closely with Congressman Bob Barr and other impeachment managers to IMPEACH criminal Bill Clinton.

name: Conservative Liberty Wire
subject: Reserve your DVD box set
body:
C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life’s most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

| This sponsored message from Conservative Liberty Wire is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=49eac64cfbde44068f20f58f6aea0f3b&_e=ossAPEGV20k2nGztJpuAUQsVKbVWo3Lzj3lo1AVZ8zaCFU_Z1AASqmuh7ZNJq_zuIIz7PXfcT6fsrRbNPhWTYVXjEy670VXEdUA8uc2_zfFrvNOHcjc2ASKb51H90Upa4oZvRwLGPFqmTb8C8dA0-R6OcK4BXacndCYkkcSoKzH7uTHjLW6iZ3pBbhrL8dSr4oN1y8KhkGw5mxJ2bK4sQIcEVrtHderR_rPiZ8zdibO2Vvxlh6K9T3nL30hpmxMckSRX-w1T7jwRCgKzXjREx98FzPm_D7IxkIM2OIJEC_3nou1WDE9dNrpmUqya-67oErXe7TKvt2oz9B91WIIxUgYj-j4F3n1LxL4XDe0_7_45eTe6-L0JFaBwk1P1p1AUsQMazLEh1M4_1ozJJToA5cqIvX3wvb4uJx3FFKxHY2DJ23YIY1lwE-4VTMQCOI_A)

| | Dear friend, C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

His signature ability to communicate the truth about Christianity has inspired countless skeptics to become believers and even more believers to renew and deepen their faith--making him the greatest Christian apologist of the twentieth century.

That's why we produced "[**C.S. Lewis on Christianity,**](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=49eac64cfbde44068f20f58f6aea0f3b&_e=ossAPEGV20k2nGztJpuAUQsVKbVWo3Lzj3lo1AVZ8zaCFU_Z1AASqmuh7ZNJq_zuIIz7PXfcT6fsrRbNPhWTYVXjEy670VXEdUA8uc2_zfFrvNOHcjc2ASKb51H90Upa4oZvRwLGPFqmTb8C8dA0-R6OcK4BXacndCYkkcSoKzH7uTHjLW6iZ3pBbhrL8dSr4oN1y8KhkGw5mxJ2bK4sQIcEVrtHderR_rPiZ8zdibO2Vvxlh6K9T3nL30hpmxMckSRX-w1T7jwRCgKzXjREx2vNhFKFR7ze-N_H6mR4yWgsZATK4mAf2zZZkV3jbY5gEgdT7r63HlT1i67bVVAQxp_lGFE322S5aIomafSX4xso1wpfejpFyqBIHhBSkpmF2rpZODpqOKusTqoe4dbZ8rctmKUY3Z6XUY-37AuUm3eXewocoo9gKcWflkJYj3_I)" which you can now own on DVD.
---
| [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=49eac64cfbde44068f20f58f6aea0f3b&_e=ossAPEGV20k2nGztJpuAUQsVKbVWo3Lzj3lo1AVZ8zaCFU_Z1AASqmuh7ZNJq_zuIIz7PXfcT6fsrRbNPhWTYVXjEy670VXEdUA8uc2_zfFrvNOHcjc2ASKb51H90Upa4oZvRwLGPFqmTb8C8dA0-R6OcK4BXacndCYkkcSoKzH7uTHjLW6iZ3pBbhrL8dSr4oN1y8KhkGw5mxJ2bK4sQIcEVrtHderR_rPiZ8zdibO2Vvxlh6K9T3nL30hpmxMckSRX-w1T7jwRCgKzXjREx5SPUBH9PWGT5dayJbQJzoda4cABKPZVecR9wqUw9p0awZZeqy3xEhQCYXOORI-K6ICtxivxsuwBBm7OFXzdQSOkk25xVp0cS5_sgDljM3FuQQRw_DY_HtkOKhhxkTqa8TU7iNgUHB7h0EWZEut7kXTYopAf_g-rLfSTkn15cx5c)
---
| | In this course you will learn Lewis's profound insights on the central topics of Christianity, including:
* good and evil,
* conversion and faith,
* prayer and the Bible,
* suffering and death,
* heaven and hell, and much more.

Dr. Ward is a Distinguished Visiting Professor at Hillsdale College and a member of the Faculty of Theology and Religion at the University of Oxford. He is the author of _Planet Narnia: The Seven Heavens in the Imagination of C.S. Lewis_ and _After Humanity: A Guide to C.S. Lewis 's The Abolition of Man_, and the co-editor of _The Cambridge Companion to C.S. Lewis_. He even spent three years living in Lewis's Oxford home as the resident Warden of The Kilns!

This course is especially moving because it examines C.S. Lewis's beautiful writings about faith and traces Lewis's own journey from skeptic to believer. The combination provides an opportunity for us to grow in our knowledge and understanding of Christianity.

That's why I hope you'll join us in examining the eternal questions of Christianity with C.S. Lewis with [this beautiful new DVD box set](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=49eac64cfbde44068f20f58f6aea0f3b&_e=ossAPEGV20k2nGztJpuAUQsVKbVWo3Lzj3lo1AVZ8zaCFU_Z1AASqmuh7ZNJq_zuIIz7PXfcT6fsrRbNPhWTYVXjEy670VXEdUA8uc2_zfFrvNOHcjc2ASKb51H90Upa4oZvRwLGPFqmTb8C8dA0-R6OcK4BXacndCYkkcSoKzH7uTHjLW6iZ3pBbhrL8dSr4oN1y8KhkGw5mxJ2bK4sQIcEVrtHderR_rPiZ8zdibO2Vvxlh6K9T3nL30hpmxMckSRX-w1T7jwRCgKzXjREx8oG00Zz0dnRIplP6W5103iW6Phag5bM3Pyb2d6AaSpgUjNJBMq7TEii47eQ4ksaTqXmNauG-RpDRMIOATLCLxu3-T4wdwS3D2SZ_NZXGyPOAnkNzoU9YzYhwgoZV5fjNDmBzyTq10hMudevTAPeCyIDk-32Y1u2nDqkUgDbWQRr).

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
---

Hundreds of GOP state legislators and officials promoted the election fraud claims that led to Trump’s January 6th insurrection. 2. Trump’s election-denying crony Daniel Kelly just advanced in Wisconsin’s pivotal Supreme Court race that will have an important impact on abortion access and elections in the state. 3. The outcome of this Supreme Court race could give us the chance to un-gerrymander Wisconsin – giving Democrats their best chance in over a decade to pick up seats in the state's legislature. State-level races are overlooked and underfunded – making them especially vulnerable to Republicans’ outsized spending. name: Kevin McCarthy
subject: With a heavy heart
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McCarthy for Congress
body:
I come bearing some bad news:

| |
---

| Hi Peter,

The Primary Election is just a year away, and we have a lot of work to do to get re-elected. [Are you in?](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=916928ae38&e=292fde286a)

We’re having a St Patrick’s Day party on March 15th at Fiddler's Green in Millbrae at 5:30pm to support my re-election. [Can you join us?](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=fa38e20551&e=292fde286a)

Here is what’s at stake: right now, Republicans are removing senior Democrats from their committee assignments, proposing carrying guns into the Capitol, and attempting to dismantle Social Security. We need to come together to preserve our democracy and end the politics of division and extremism in Washington DC.

[Will you make a contribution to my re-election today?](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=eccff5ef5f&e=292fde286a)

I am honored to represent you as your Congressman. [I hope to see you on March 15th in Millbrae!](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=fdb519fcd5&e=292fde286a)
---
| Sincerely,
Kevin Mullin

name: Brie Bristol
subject: The Wire: Happy Women's History Month!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee Massachusetts Democratic Party
body:
| | |
---
| | _**Our goal with the MassDems Wire is to give you the tools to boost Democrats and take on the GOP and their Republican allies. Use this information on social media, as the basis for a letter to the editor of your local paper, in emails to your personal network, or in conversation with your friends and neighbors.**_
---
|
---
| | Dear Peter, Women's History Month is a celebration of the achievements and contributions of women throughout history. It originated as a week-long event in the United States in 1981, and now it extends into the entire month of March. The importance of Women's History Month lies in its ability to highlight the often overlooked role that women have played in shaping the world we live in. This month provides an opportunity to recognize the courage, strength, and resilience of women who have paved the way for future generations. When it comes to women in Massachusetts politics, we’ve broken glass ceilings again and again. In recent years, the Commonwealth has elected Elizabeth Warren, Ayanna Pressley, Andrea Campbell, Donna Buckley, and our newest gubernatorial pair Gov. Maura Healey and Lt. Gov. Kim Driscoll to name a few. These women have broken barriers and served as role models for future generations of female politicians. Below, this email is jam packed with information about Democratic women crushing politics nationally and locally in the Commonwealth. Further down, you will find ways to take action on social media or in your conversations with peers — as well as [creative assets](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59466470/394334076/1968869156?id=1C5FHSLdGKLiyXcBscLQqFRt7OzKqk2Ov&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzgwOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjY2ZmE2ZC03Y2I4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CvuwfrsXBcpauVC1G27w2jZ0YTiUH7of7yrLQ6y8ImU=&emci=aa61a979-84b7-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=bf66fa6d-7cb8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=17281370) for use.
**Please forward this email to your networks and don't hesitate to reach out to me directly at brie@massdems.org about using the guidance below.** Happy Women’s History Month! Sincerely,

**Brie Bristol**
Digital Content Manager
Massachusetts Democratic Party
---
| | |
---
| |
# **Women in Politics: Nationally**
---
| | Women's history is a tapestry of immense importance that weaves its way through our national identity, especially in the realm of politics. It is crucial to acknowledge the contributions of women throughout history to comprehend our country's past and present. Although women have made incredible strides in achieving representation, they have faced significant obstacles, particularly women of color and members of marginalized communities. With Women's History Month, we have the perfect opportunity to honor and celebrate the remarkable contributions of women who have paved the way for generations to come. Visionary trailblazers such as Shirley Chisholm, the first Black woman elected to Congress, and Ruth Bader Ginsburg, the second woman to serve on the Supreme Court, have left indelible marks on our history. We are fortunate to witness the triumph of the tireless efforts of women. For instance, we have the first female Vice President, Kamala Harris, in the US, along with 25 female Senators and 124 women in the U.S. House of Representatives. Even the U.S. Supreme Court has four women serving, including Associate Justice Kentanji Brown Jackson, the first Black woman to hold the position. Furthermore, state governments across the country have women in powerful positions. Women currently hold 24% of Governor positions, 48.8% of Lt. Governor positions, 30.1% of State Senate positions, and 33.7% of State House/Assembly positions. These remarkable achievements are a testament to the tenacity and resilience of women everywhere. Today, none of this could be possible without the tireless efforts of women throughout history, not just in our neighborhoods but internationally. By recognizing the challenges women have faced and overcome, we can work towards building a more equitable future. We must honor the struggles and triumphs of women everywhere and continue to promote gender equality and support women's rights through our policies and actions.
---
| | |
---
| |
# **Women in Politics: Locally**
---
| | Have you ever wondered who are the female politicians making a difference in Massachusetts? Well, with the state-wide election last year, we have more women in elected positions than ever before in our state, and they are shaking things up! In the current 2023-2024 Legislative Session, women hold 30% of the MA State Senate seats, with 12 powerful members leading the way. On the House side, women make up 32% of the MA House of Representatives, with 51 brilliant members dedicated to serving their communities. But the excitement doesn't end there - in fact, women now hold five out of the six top spots on the ticket, creating the first-ever all-women Governor and Lt. Governor pairing. We couldn't be prouder of our female identifying lawmakers, and we want you to get to know them too! **Check out the list below, find your Democratic representatives, and visit their website to learn more about how they are making a difference in your community. ** Let's celebrate the power of women in politics!
For more statistics, [click here ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59466473/394334078/79738228?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzgwOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjY2ZmE2ZC03Y2I4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CvuwfrsXBcpauVC1G27w2jZ0YTiUH7of7yrLQ6y8ImU=&emci=aa61a979-84b7-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=bf66fa6d-7cb8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=17281370)for further information from the MA Women’s Caucus.
---

# **Ways to Take Action**
---
| |
* Post on social media by recording a short video about what Women’s History Month means to you. Post a direct-to-camera (selfie!) video on why female representation in politics matters, don’t forget to include hashtags like #Women’sHistoryMonth, #Herstory, or #WomenInPolitics

* Use your phone to film yourself vertically from the chest up.
* Record in a well lit room or in front of a window. If possible, try to find a neutral wall or background.
* Make sure you’re in a quiet area and avoid sources of background noise like cars, parks, bodies of water, etc.
* Record less than 30 seconds. Don’t be afraid to record multiple takes to get a clean video!
* Post them on Twitter, Facebook, or Instagram, ask your friends to uplift these posts, and don’t hesitate to flag them for us by DMing @massdems.
* After you film your selfie video, use the sample posts and creative assets below to keep spreading the word about the Women’s History Month online:

Let's continue to uplift and empower the women in our lives, and work towards a world where gender-based discrimination is a thing of the past. _Post #4_ Massachusetts has been home to some of the most influential women in American politics. This Women's History Month, let's honor their legacies and continue to support women leaders in our communities. _Post #5_ Glad to be celebrating the achievements and contributions of women during #Women'sHistoryMonth!

clean_body: | | | | | Our goal with the MassDems Wire is to give you the tools to boost Democrats and take on the GOP and their Republican allies. Use this information on social media, as the basis for a letter to the editor of your local paper, in emails to your personal network, or in conversation with your friends and neighbors. | | | Dear Peter, Women's History Month is a celebration of the achievements and contributions of women throughout history. It originated as a week-long event in the United States in 1981, and now it extends into the entire month of March. The importance of Women's History Month lies in its ability to highlight the often overlooked role that women have played in shaping the world we live in. This month provides an opportunity to recognize the courage, strength, and resilience of women who have paved the way for future generations. When it comes to women in Massachusetts politics, we’ve broken glass ceilings again and again. In recent years, the Commonwealth has elected Elizabeth Warren, Ayanna Pressley, Andrea Campbell, Donna Buckley, and our newest gubernatorial pair Gov. Maura Healey and Lt. Gov. Kim Driscoll to name a few. These women have broken barriers and served as role models for future generations of female politicians. Below, this email is jam packed with information about Democratic women crushing politics nationally and locally in the Commonwealth. Further down, you will find ways to take action on social media or in your conversations with peers — as well as for use. Please forward this email to your networks and don't hesitate to reach out to me directly at brie@massdems.org about using the guidance below. Happy Women’s History Month! Sincerely, Brie Bristol Digital Content Manager Massachusetts Democratic Party | | | | | Women in Politics: Nationally | | Women's history is a tapestry of immense importance that weaves its way through our national identity, especially in the realm of politics. It is crucial to acknowledge the contributions of women throughout history to comprehend our country's past and present. Although women have made incredible strides in achieving representation, they have faced significant obstacles, particularly women of color and members of marginalized communities. With Women's History Month, we have the perfect opportunity to honor and celebrate the remarkable contributions of women who have paved the way for generations to come. Visionary trailblazers such as Shirley Chisholm, the first Black woman elected to Congress, and Ruth Bader Ginsburg, the second woman to serve on the Supreme Court, have left indelible marks on our history. We are fortunate to witness the triumph of the tireless efforts of women. For instance, we have the first female Vice President, Kamala Harris, in the US, along with 25 female Senators and 124 women in the U.S. House of Representatives. Even the U.S. Supreme Court has four women serving, including Associate Justice Kentanji Brown Jackson, the first Black woman to hold the position. Furthermore, state governments across the country have women in powerful positions. Women currently hold 24% of Governor positions, 48.8% of Lt. Governor positions, 30.1% of State Senate positions, and 33.7% of State House/Assembly positions. These remarkable achievements are a testament to the tenacity and resilience of women everywhere. Today, none of this could be possible without the tireless efforts of women throughout history, not just in our neighborhoods but internationally. By recognizing the challenges women have faced and overcome, we can work towards building a more equitable future. We must honor the struggles and triumphs of women everywhere and continue to promote gender equality and support women's rights through our policies and actions. | | | | | Women in Politics: Locally | | Have you ever wondered who are the female politicians making a difference in Massachusetts? Well, with the state-wide election last year, we have more women in elected positions than ever before in our state, and they are shaking things up! In the current 2023-2024 Legislative Session, women hold 30% of the MA State Senate seats, with 12 powerful members leading the way. On the House side, women make up 32% of the MA House of Representatives, with 51 brilliant members dedicated to serving their communities. But the excitement doesn't end there - in fact, women now hold five out of the six top spots on the ticket, creating the first-ever all-women Governor and Lt. Governor pairing. We couldn't be prouder of our female identifying lawmakers, and we want you to get to know them too! Check out the list below, find your Democratic representatives, and visit their website to learn more about how they are making a difference in your community. Let's celebrate the power of women in politics! | | Change takes courage.

It’s something I’ve learned in both business and government, and it’s something I knew bringing new conservative solutions to Florida as governor.
Now, I’m taking on the Washington insiders to change the way Congress works. We have to STOP business as usual in Washington and that starts by making our government work for YOU, not the Washington insiders.
_Because I have advocated for change, they have put a target on my back._
**I don’t care whose toes I step on. I wasn't elected to represent the Washington insiders - I was elected to represent the people of Florida.**

body:
This radical bill completely tramples on parental rights and harms children! Contact Senate leadership and tell them to STOP this bad bill!

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .

Steve Johnson
Communications Director

name: Kadida Kenner
subject: Our freedom to vote is still under attack
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project_
body:
I need your help to expand the electorate and defend our democracy.

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59468064/394338454/409185284?refcode=EM_FR_230301EOM2_EOM_KK_FULL_5_B_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM1MGI3MTY5LTgzYjgtZWQxMS1hOGUwLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=mlf7X6jx1KQsA-wb8fbZSD7AhEHrmhRO1ukU5ugsIzc=)
| Fellow activist, as the founding executive director of a voting rights organization, we need to talk about the reality of how additional restrictive voter ID laws will make it harder for many Pennsylvanians to vote and will directly affect the communities we center. Pennsylvanians must reject restrictive voter ID laws.

To instill faith in our elections, Pennsylvania lawmakers should pass legislation for same-day voter registration like in North Carolina, and early voting like Georgians are lucky to have. Making the ballot more accessible, and the act of voting quicker and simpler should be the goal, not restrictive voter ID laws advocated for by those who fall for Trump’s Big Lies.

Pennsylvania already has voter ID laws on the books, and they’re effective. The truth is, any additional voter ID laws are solutions in search of a problem. The Pennsylvania electorate made their voices heard last November. They want an increase to the $7.25/hour minimum wage, fair education funding for our public schools, affordable housing and the protection and expansion of our voting rights. All of our elected officials should work on those issues first and foremost.

Additional voter identification laws are a part of an ongoing strategy to roll back decades of progress regarding expanded voting rights. One of the most effective methods we have to protect our freedom to vote **_right now_** is continuing to expand the electorate and advocate for policies that make it easier for constituents to vote – not more challenging. **[That’s why I’m writing you today to inform you we missed our end-of-month goal to raise $965 in March to hit our $2,500 February goal and continue raising the funds we need to do this work. Will you donate $10 right now to help us close that gap before the clock hits clean_body: I need your help to expand the electorate and defend our democracy. = | Fellow activist, as the founding executive director of a voting rights organization, we need to talk about the reality of how additional restrictive voter ID laws will make it harder for many Pennsylvanians to vote and will directly affect the communities we center. Pennsylvanians must reject restrictive voter ID laws. To instill faith in our elections, Pennsylvania lawmakers should pass legislation for same-day voter registration like in North Carolina, and early voting like Georgians are lucky to have. Making the ballot more accessible, and the act of voting quicker and simpler should be the goal, not restrictive voter ID laws advocated for by those who fall for Trump’s Big Lies. Pennsylvania already has voter ID laws on the books, and they’re effective. The truth is, any additional voter ID laws are solutions in search of a problem. The Pennsylvania electorate made their voices heard last November. They want an increase to the $7.25/hour minimum wage, fair education funding for our public schools, affordable housing and the protection and expansion of our voting rights. All of our elected officials should work on those issues first and foremost. Additional voter identification laws are a part of an ongoing strategy to roll back decades of progress regarding expanded voting rights. One of the most effective methods we have to protect our freedom to vote right now is continuing to expand the electorate and advocate for policies that make it easier for constituents to vote – not more challenging. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. = = = = In solidarity, Kadida Kenner Chief Executive Officer The New Pennsylvania Project = | The New Pennsylvania Project was founded in May 2021 to expand Pennsylvania’s electorate, so it reflects the Commonwealth’s demographically changing population. In the next four years, NPP will engage and empower young residents and those living in underrepresented immigrant communities and communities of color. We register communities to vote, provide civic education about the issues that matter in their neighborhoods, and mobilize Pennsylvanians to vote in every election. = PA ELECTION DATES : Municipal Primary: May 16, 2023 Last day to =: May 1, 2023 Last day to =: May 9, 2023 Municipal Election: November 7, 2023 Last day to =: October 23, 2023 Last day to =: October 31, 2023 || || || New Pennsylvania Project PO Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please. to receive fewer emails from us. to stop receiving any fundraising emails from us. name: Becca Balint
subject: We need student loan forgiveness
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont, P
body:
Yesterday, I was proud to stand alongside activists and fellow members of Congress in front of the Supreme Court to advocate for student loan forgiveness.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59468209/394338888/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NDA5YzI4ZC04NGI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NSomiJvq6CrKZgXVwjhBwJB-xoNEBTRloJxN703dJUY=&emci=c0b99de7-74b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=8409c28d-84b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) | Dear Peter, **Yesterday, I was proud to stand alongside activists and fellow members of Congress in front of the Supreme Court to advocate for student loan forgiveness.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59468210/394338889/1221753665?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NDA5YzI4ZC04NGI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NSomiJvq6CrKZgXVwjhBwJB-xoNEBTRloJxN703dJUY=&emci=c0b99de7-74b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=8409c28d-84b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=43402272) SCOTUS heard oral arguments yesterday in opposition to President Biden’s plan to eliminate up to $20,000 per borrower in federal student loans. **Let’s be clear: Biden’s action to implement mass student debt relief will liberate thousands of Vermonters from back-breaking interest payments.** An entire generation of people are being held back by crushing student loan debt. These companies have profited off of young people who wanted to get an education. As a former public school teacher, I know firsthand that there is no greater equalizer in our society than a quality education. But right now, generations of Americans are being punished for their desire to access the opportunities that higher education provides. **We cannot let the Supreme Court un-do the tremendous progress made by President Biden.[_That’s why I'm circulating a petition calling on SCOTUS to approve Biden’s debt-relief plan. clean_body: Yesterday, I was proud to stand alongside activists and fellow members of Congress in front of the Supreme Court to advocate for student loan forgiveness. | | Dear Peter, Yesterday, I was proud to stand alongside activists and fellow members of Congress in front of the Supreme Court to advocate for student loan forgiveness. SCOTUS heard oral arguments yesterday in opposition to President Biden’s plan to eliminate up to $20,000 per borrower in federal student loans. Let’s be clear: Biden’s action to implement mass student debt relief will liberate thousands of Vermonters from back-breaking interest payments. An entire generation of people are being held back by crushing student loan debt. These companies have profited off of young people who wanted to get an education. As a former public school teacher, I know firsthand that there is no greater equalizer in our society than a quality education. But right now, generations of Americans are being punished for their desire to access the opportunities that higher education provides. We cannot let the Supreme Court un-do the tremendous progress made by President Biden. In solidarity, Becca = If you'd like to receive fewer emails and only see our most important updates, **If you'd rather not stay up to date with Becca's work, you can . | [](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/15781?t=6&akid=1703%2E564421%2EGgOq3u) I’ve got some bad news:

In the dead of night, Kevin McCarthy and the Republican Party did what they’ve threatened for the last two years.

**They removed me from the House Intelligence Committee where I have served as Chairman and the top Democrat for years.**

This is political retribution for my leading the impeachment of Donald Trump, pure and simple, and a dangerous effort to go after anyone who holds them accountable. It also turns the Intelligence Committee into a political plaything for their right-wing supporters.

FOlks, I really need you with me to help respond to this baseless attack on my ability to serve my constituents and the American people.

[**Please, contribute $3 whatever you can spare today to our campaign and Ron Be Gone to help me respond to Republicans’ baseless attacks on my ability to hold them accountable. This isn’t the end of my fight for truth, accountability, and our democracy. In fact, it’s only the start: »**](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/15783?t=8&akid=1703%2E564421%2EGgOq3u) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Ron Be Gone and Adam Schiff will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $3 »](https://act.ronbegone.com/go/15789?t=14&akid=1703%2E564421%2EGgOq3u)

Here’s a quick reminder: Momentum is on our side. |
#
Hi Peter, it’s Ben Wikler, the Chair of the Democratic Party of Wisconsin. [**A******head** of our next fundraising deadline, I’m rushing for your help to elect pro-democracy Judge Janet Protasiewicz to our Supreme Court and support Tammy Baldwin for Senate.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59468614/394340351/577604170?refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230301_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83Njk1MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzhkMTkxMi04NWI4LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H8vBvCAQUYEKz9cCRFk80twZRf_MIn6u9CQpwcnhuJk=) Janet Protasiewicz — _our progressive nominee for state Supreme Court_ — tallied more votes in last Tuesday's primary than the two MAGA extremists on the ballot combined. The momentum is on our side to elect the commonsense candidate in this race, but we can’t for a microsecond get overconfident. name: =?utf-8?Q?Carmen=20Goers?=
subject: A train ticket should come with peace of mind, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carmen for Congress]( "Paid for by Carmen for Congress")
body:
Transparency and safety are required | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bc3a573663c4/im-announcing-my-run-for-us-congress-in-the-8th-congressional-district-13539767?e=0e51d1e991)
---
| | [ ](https://mcshanellc.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ac7c5348d309f6a3d3cffd39b&id=7e73c955e8&e=0e51d1e991)
---
| | Peter,
---
| As the nation witnesses the horrific sights of the Ohio tragedy, I’ve listened to the concerns of Washingtonians about similar catastrophes we’ve faced.

Considering the train derailment incidents _we’ve faced_ over the last few years in the Evergreen State, such unease is warranted!

I remember watching in 2017 when an Amtrak derailed, and a passenger shared that the emergency doors wouldn’t work, and windows had to be broken to escape.
---
| [ ](https://mcshanellc.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ac7c5348d309f6a3d3cffd39b&id=43035b2d44&e=0e51d1e991)
---
| In 2020, a train carrying crude oil was purposefully derailed by activist terrorists, filling our Washington skies with black smoke.

**_I never thought I’d witness the federal government refusing to inform Americans about the presence of hazardous chemicals on trains on the basis of ‘national security’_ ,** _or the deficient response that we’re seeing take place in Ohio now._ The purpose of government is to protect its population, and
we must demand accountability for it!
I’m running for Congress to take a stand for responsible safety and security measures _that are transparent and protect the lives of every Washingtonian_ , Peter.

Purchasing a train ticket should come with the peace of mind that our federal government has performed the due diligence to ensure that destinations are reached safely!

**I can’t turn this federal train wreck of mishaps around on my own, Peter****.** [**I need your help to get to DC, and your monthly donation of even $15 will help send the message that Washingtonians expect federal officials to prioritize the safety of Americans in our streets, railways, airspace, and in our communities!**](https://mcshanellc.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ac7c5348d309f6a3d3cffd39b&id=210eaa2a8d&e=0e51d1e991)

Please support my mission to make government transparent and accountable!
---
| [DONATE $25 »](https://mcshanellc.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ac7c5348d309f6a3d3cffd39b&id=b7047a32c1&e=0e51d1e991 "DONATE $25 »")

Please support my mission to make government transparent and accountable! | "DONATE $25 »" | "DONATE $50 »" | "DONATE $100 »" | "DONATE $500 »" | "DONATE $1000»" | "DONATE ANY AMOUNT »" | For Washington, | | Carmen Goers Candidate for U.S. Congress, WA-8 | | | | | | | | | | | | "Paid for by Carmen for Congress" | | Copyright © 2023 Carmen for Congress, All rights reserved.

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter, did you
see? Yesterday, the Supreme Court began hearing arguments for Republicans’
sham lawsuits against the President’s student debt relief plan.

Justice Democrats Cori Bush, Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib, Ayanna Pressley,
Jamaal Bowman, and Pramila Jayapal were there — rallying with protestors
to affirm how critical student debt cancellation is for racial and
economic justice.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129724?refcode=em20230301-27780&t=1001&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ][IMG]

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129724?refcode=em20230301-27780&t=1002&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ][IMG]

From day one of their campaigns to yesterday’s rally, JD incumbents have
led the charge for student debt relief, building a core progressive
coalition that is ready to do whatever it takes to cancel student debt.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129724?refcode=em20230301-27780&t=1003&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ]Will you stand with our Justice Democrats as they fight for justice for
millions of borrowers — and make sure we can elect the next generation of
progressive champions who will keep the movement going?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129724?refcode=em20230301-27780&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ]Contribute $3

When JD launched, people said student debt relief was a pipe dream — now
43 million borrowers could get debt canceled. That’s the impact of our
work — making the impossible possible and electing working class leaders
who will champion transformative policies that put resources back into our
communities.

For over six years, we’ve been transforming the Democratic Party by
recruiting, supporting, and electing radical, diverse slates of working
class leaders who will do what it takes to bring justice for our people.
That means student debt relief now — and it also means Medicare for All, a
Green New Deal, workers’ rights, reproductive justice, getting corporate
money out of politics, and more.

We’re here to make these policies a reality by finding and electing the
next slate of Justice Democrats, and we need your help to do it.
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129724?refcode=em20230301-27780&t=1023&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ]Peter,
will you donate $3 today
so we can build progressive power and elect progressive champions who will
fight for critical policies like student debt relief?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129724?refcode=em20230301-27780&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1024&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ]Contribute »

— Team JD

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1025&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1026&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230301-27780-footer&t=1027&refcode2=27780_1203867_15kXKb&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1028&akid=27780%2E1203867%2E15kXKb

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: CaucusRoom
subject: Why is Yellen in Ukraine?
body:
Teacher's Union has a melt down. No images? [Click here](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-e-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-s/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-r/)
## **Happy Wednesday! Join Adam and Connie [_tonight at 6 PM MST_](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-y/) to chat about headlines in the news. **
## **Here are some recent articles shared by CaucusRoom members. **
[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-j/) [Schedule a CaucusRoom Live demo today!](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-t/) **Teachers Union Melt Down** Teachers Union President Weingarten[ **railed against challenges to Biden 's student loan cancellation plan outside of the Supreme Court**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-i/) as oral arguments began for two cases that could put the proposal on ice. **Afghanistan Withdrawal Left $7B Behind** A government watchdog group reports that **[the Biden administration left $7.2 billion worth of U.S. military equipment under Taliban control](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-d/)** when it abruptly left Afghanistan in 2021. **Why is Yellen in Ukraine?** Treasury Secretary**[Janet Yellen made a previously unannounced visit to Kyiv on Monday](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-h/) **intended to highlight the U.S.' enduring support for the country days after the one-year anniversary of Russia's invasion. **Wind Farms are Harmful to Animals** A [**senior Biden administration scientist authored an internal memo warning of the impacts offshore wind development may have on marine life** ](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-k/)months before the recent spate of whale deaths along the East Coast. Twenty-three dead whales have washed up on the east coast since December! **Fact Check: False** Biden made a controversial declaration during a Black History Month event Monday,**[saying while he might be a "White boy," he isn't "stupid."](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-u/)** **Military Records Leaked** Rep. Anna Paulina Luna **[speculated that her private military records might have been leaked to a Democratic opposition](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-o/)** research firm by the U.S. Air Force.

**Let****'s[ have a good laugh](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-x/)!**
* **[Do not recommend](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-m/)**.
* I'm **[negative](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-c/).**
* He's the**[village idiot](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-q/).**
* Your**[spelling lesson](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-a/) **for the day.

[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-f/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-z/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-v/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-e/) | CaucusRoom, LLC
800 Washington Ave Ste C
Golden, CO 80401 You are receiving this email because you are a valued member of CaucusRoom [Preferences](https://caucusroom.updatemyprofile.com/i-qkrltjy-6F882DBF-trhiujldw-g) | [Unsubscribe](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-u-qkrltjy-trhiujldw-w/)
